claims.claim: Moderate egg consumption (up to one egg per day) is not associated with cardiovascular disease risk overall.
High egg consumption was associated with a higher risk of cardiovascular disease in people with type 2 diabetes.
The association between egg consumption and cardiovascular disease risk was found to be lower in Asian cohorts compared to US and European cohorts.
The study's findings may not be generalizable to other populations because the cohorts included health professionals.
